sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.103 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: (V.\,V.\,Uspenskii). A Hausdorff topological group$G$is called if it does not admit a strictly coarser Hausdorff group topology. A topological group is called if its two-sided uniform structure is complete. It is known that a finite direct product of Raikov complete minimal topological groups is again minimal. Is it true that an arbitrary Cartesian product of Raikov complete minimal topological gr...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
